title: Parkio Daily for May 12, 2026
teaser: Halloween party tickets sell out fast, EPCOT crowds build this weekend, and a classic attraction pauses briefly for updates.

rides.name: Soarin'
rides.note: Closing May 14 for temporary update—ride before Wednesday

rides.name: Space Mountain
rides.note: Early morning tends to offer shortest waits

rides.name: Test Track
rides.note: Reopened with reimagined theme—check wait times before noon

breaking.title: Halloween Party Sells Out for October 31
breaking.body: Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween Party tickets for Halloween night sold out within hours of going on sale this morning. The October 31 date—falling on a Saturday this year—is no longer available, though other August through October dates remain open with pricing from $119 to $229.

parkioInsight.text: Alternative party dates in late October tend to offer similar entertainment with better availability. Booking mid-week dates often means lower pricing and easier access to trick-or-treat stops.

bignews.title: Walt Disney World President Announces Retirement
bignews.body: Jeff Vahle, current president of Walt Disney World Resort, will retire this summer after several years leading operations. The announcement follows recent Parks & Resorts leadership changes across the Walt Disney Company, though no successor has been named yet.

parkioInsight.text: Leadership transitions at this level rarely impact day-to-day park operations immediately. Guests visiting in the next few months are unlikely to notice changes tied to this shift.

bignews.title: EPCOT Park Reservations Fill Up for Weekend
bignews.body: Park reservations for ticket holders are sold out at EPCOT this coming Saturday and Sunday. The Flower & Garden Festival continues through May, drawing higher-than-usual weekend crowds to the park.

parkioInsight.text: EPCOT festival weekends usually see heavier traffic near World Showcase after 11am. Riding Remy's Ratatouille Adventure or Guardians of the Galaxy before 10am typically avoids peak waits.

topstories.title: Soarin' Pauses May 14 for Brief Update
topstories.body: The flight simulator attraction at EPCOT will close May 14 for a temporary update, giving guests just two more days to experience the current version. Disney has indicated the closure will last only a short time, with the attraction returning later this month in a refreshed format.

parkioInsight.text: If you want to ride the current version before it changes, visit before Wednesday evening. Soarin' wait times tend to drop after 7pm during Flower & Garden.

topstories.title: Old Navy x Disney Summer Collection Launches May 13
topstories.body: A new Americana-themed Mickey & Friends apparel line arrives in Old Navy stores and online tomorrow. The collection includes tees, sweatshirts, and matching sets for all ages with nostalgic summer graphics.

parkioInsight.text: These collabs often sell out quickly in popular sizes online. Shopping early tomorrow morning usually offers the best selection before items go out of stock.

icymi.title: Kate Mara Visits Magic Kingdom for Mother's Day
icymi.body: The actress shared Instagram photos from her Mother's Day visit to Walt Disney World, thanking the resort for 'magical adventures' with her family.

spotlight.title: Early Entry Strategy for Peak Crowd Days
spotlight.body: A recent Disney Tourist Blog photo report documents a challenging Early Entry morning at Hollywood Studios during a 10/10 crowd day, showing how even resort guest perks can be overwhelmed during peak periods. The experience highlights the importance of having backup plans when first-choice attractions hit capacity early.

parkioInsight.text: On high-crowd days, pivoting to lower-demand areas during Early Entry often yields better results than queuing for the most popular rides. Consider hitting Sunset Boulevard attractions if Galaxy's Edge fills up fast.
